commentaries.author: George Leo Haydock
commentaries.content:
The meanest in Manasses Mark how the Lord chooses the humble (who are mean and little in their own eyes) for the greatest enterprises. (Challoner)
Hebrew and Septuagint literally, "My millenary is poor, or lowly "This term means a great family, from which many others spring, or a city inhabited by such. Bethlehem was of this description in Juda, Micheas v. 2. Ephra and the family of Abiezer were not the first in Manasses. Grotius observes, that Gedeon and Cincinnatus were called to the highest offices, when they least expected it.

commentaries.author: Richard Challoner
commentaries.content: The meanest in Manasses: Mark how the Lord chooseth the humble (who are mean and little in their own eyes) for the greatest enterprises.
